name: Ascend Configuration Management
description: Two-tier configuration system for Ascend that manages defaults and user overrides in ~/.ascend/config.yaml. Provides loading, saving, and individual key access to configuration values with type coercion support.

types.name: AscendConfig

fields.name: anthropic_api_key_env
fields.description: Environment variable name for Anthropic API key

fields.name: default_lookback_hours
fields.description: Default lookback hours for data queries

fields.name: linear_api_key_env
fields.description: Environment variable name for Linear API key

fields.name: slack_channels
fields.description: List of Slack channels to monitor

fields.name: slack_bot_token_env
fields.description: Environment variable name for Slack bot token

types.description: Pydantic model representing Ascend configuration with defaults for directories, API keys, model selection, and integration settings

functions.name: load_config
functions.description: Load configuration from YAML file or return default AscendConfig if file does not exist. Filters out None values from loaded YAML.

inputs.name: config_path
inputs.description: Optional path to config file; defaults to CONFIG_PATH (~/.ascend/config.yaml)

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: File exists but cannot be read due to permissions or IO issues

postconditions: If config_path exists, loads YAML and merges with defaults
If config_path does not exist, returns AscendConfig with all defaults

structured_side_effects.description: Reads YAML configuration file if it exists

functions.name: save_config
functions.description: Save AscendConfig instance to YAML file. Creates parent directories if they don't exist.

inputs.name: config_path
inputs.description: Optional path to config file; defaults to CONFIG_PATH (~/.ascend/config.yaml)

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Cannot write to file due to permissions or IO issues

preconditions: cfg must be valid AscendConfig instance
postconditions: Config file exists at specified path
Parent directories are created if they did not exist
YAML file contains serialized configuration with no flow style and unsorted keys

structured_side_effects.description: Writes YAML configuration file and creates parent directories

functions.name: get_config_value
functions.description: Get a single configuration value by key. Loads config if not provided. Returns string representation of the value.

inputs.name: cfg
inputs.description: Optional configuration object; loads from default path if None

error_cases.name: unknown_key
error_cases.condition: key is not a valid field in AscendConfig

error_cases.name: load_errors
error_cases.condition: cfg is None and load_config fails

preconditions: key must exist in AscendConfig model
postconditions: Returns string representation of config value

structured_side_effects.description: Reads config file if cfg parameter is None

functions.name: set_config_value
functions.description: Set a single configuration value by key with automatic type coercion (int, list, or string), then save to file. Returns updated config.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: String value to set (will be coerced to target type)

inputs.name: config_path
inputs.description: Optional path to config file; defaults to CONFIG_PATH

error_cases.name: unknown_key
error_cases.condition: key is not a valid field in AscendConfig

error_cases.name: type_coercion_error
error_cases.condition: Cannot coerce value to target type (e.g., int() fails)

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Cannot read or write config file

preconditions: key must exist in AscendConfig model
postconditions: Config value is updated and persisted to disk
Integer values are coerced from string using int()
List values are coerced by splitting on comma and stripping whitespace

invariants: _DEFAULTS dictionary contains immutable default values for config keys
AscendConfig always validates fields through Pydantic BaseModel
Config files are always stored as YAML
Type coercion in set_config_value follows pattern: int → int(), list → comma-split, other → str

data_access.rationale: Configuration module manages persistent user settings in YAML format on local filesystem

side_effects.rationale: Writes user configuration to ~/.ascend/config.yaml with directory creation

authority.rationale: This module is the authoritative source for Ascend configuration management, including loading, saving, and accessing config values
